findings.title: PDF Expenses 'Category' column prints raw category IDs (UUIDs), not names
findings.rootCause: _expenses_section renders `cat = e.get('category_id') or e.get('category') or tr('exp_uncategorised')` and prints it raw (_render.py:684,688). Expenses store `categoryId` as the category's `id` (ManualTab.tsx:565 `value={c.id}`), and user-created categories get `id: generateId()` (Personalization.tsx:52) — an opaque UUID, not a slug. The route already builds a category id→name map `_cat_name` for the BUDGET section (__init__.py:1114-1118) but never passes it to `_expenses_section`, and the SELECT for expenses (__init__.py:1153-1158) fetches only `category_id`, not a joined name. The app's own budget-title code (budgets/helpers.ts:226-227) does id-then-name resolution precisely to avoid this; the PDF expenses path skips it.

findings.title: PDF Settle-up seeds a phantom full-name person for owner-involved settlements, overstating debts vs the in-app screen
findings.rootCause: Settlements snapshot `from_name`/`to_name` as `users.name` — the FULL display name (settlements.py:338). The roster the PDF builds (and the app uses) keys expenses by the companion's first-name token (the owner is self-stamped under first name). The app's settle math reconciles this via an explicit first-name-token fallback in resolveSettlementParties (balances.ts:107-122). The PDF's `_settle_section` settlement loop (_render.py:840-861) does plain exact-match: `if frm not in bal: bal[frm] = 0.0; bal[frm] += amt` — so a full-name snapshot that doesn't exact-match the roster SEEDS A NEW phantom entry instead of folding into the existing first-name balance. Expenses balance correctly (they use roster names) but the recorded settlement lands on the phantom, leaving the real debt outstanding.

findings.title: Trip-wide photos tagged to a day are embedded twice (in the day card AND the photo gallery)
findings.rootCause: The route merges trip-level photos that carry a `dayId` into each day's `photos` list so the day cards render them (__init__.py:1076-1088). But the standalone gallery builds from `trip_photos` = the FULL photos_json list (__init__.py:251 → used at :893 `_collect_photos(trip_photos, ...)`), with no exclusion of the dayId-tagged entries that were just inlined. So a photo with a dayId is collected for both the day card and the gallery.

findings.title: Per-day map labels and budget scope labels render in English regardless of locale
findings.rootCause: The whole PDF was localised via the `_STRINGS` table (PDF-5), but these two paths hardcode English literals. __init__.py:505 builds the kicker with a raw f-string + 'PIN'/'PINS'. __init__.py:1119-1130 derives the budget label using literal 'Overall' (:1128), 'Category budget' (:1126,:1130) — even though the i18n table already defines `budget_overall` and `budget_category` keys in all four locales (_i18n.py:62-63 and equivalents), which were clearly added to be used here but never wired up.

findings.title: Cover 'SPEND' stat truncates the total instead of rounding (€842.90 shows as €842)
findings.rootCause: `stats.append((f"€{tr.num(int(trip_row['total_spend_eur']), 0)}", ...))` (__init__.py:360) casts to `int()` first, which truncates toward zero, THEN formats with 0 decimals. `tr.num(value, 0)` already rounds-to-nearest via Python format spec, so the extra `int()` both is redundant and forces truncation instead of rounding.

findings.title: 'Per-day maps' checkbox is silently ignored when 'Day-by-day plan' is unchecked
findings.rootCause: In the builder, both the overview pins map (__init__.py:469) and the per-day mini-map (__init__.py:523) are nested INSIDE `if opt('includeDays') and days_renderable:` (__init__.py:448). So `includeDayPins` is only honoured when `includeDays` is also true. The modal (modals/pdf.ts:66-69) presents the two as independent toggles, creating a state the backend silently drops.

findings.title: AI-plan PDF section is unreachable dead code carrying untranslated English (latent)
findings.rootCause: The frontend modal collects only `input[type=checkbox]` values + `options.locale` (modals/pdf.ts:133-142); no code path populates `options.aiPlan`. The builder's `ai_plan_extra` branch therefore always gets an empty list in production. The dead branch also predates/ignores the PDF-5 i18n work for its inline strings.
